perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, RevMed hereby grants to Sanofi an exclusive (even as to RevMed and its Affiliates), royalty-bearing license (which shall be sub-licensable solely as provided in Section 3.4) under the RevMed Licensed Technology, to Research, Develop, Manufacture, use, sell, offer for sale, import and otherwise Commercialize and exploit Products (including, for clarity, any Companion Diagnostics with respect to such Products) in the Field in the Licensed Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, RevMed hereby grants to Sanofi a non-exclusive, royalty-bearing license (which shall be sub-licensable solely as provided in Section 3.4) under the RevMed Licensed Technology, to Research, Develop, Manufacture, use, sell, offer for sale, import and otherwise Commercialize and exploit Products (including, for clarity, any Companion Diagnostics with respect to such Products) in the Field in the Licensed Territory.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the license granted to Sanofi in Section 3.1 from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive'. This contradicts the original intention of granting Sanofi exclusive rights, potentially impacting other sections of the agreement that rely on Sanofi having exclusive control over certain aspects of the collaboration. In particular, this contradicts Sanofi's sole responsibility for commercialization in Section 8.1, as a non-exclusive license would imply RevMed can commercialize the same products. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 8.7 and unless otherwise delegated to RevMed by the JCC, Sanofi shall have the sole right and responsibility, at its own expense, for all aspects of the Commercialization of the Products in the Field in the Licensed Territory including: (a) developing and executing a commercial launch and pre-launch plan, (b) negotiating with applicable Governmental Authorities regarding the pricing and reimbursement status of the Products; (c) marketing and promotion (including promotional materials); (d) booking sales and distribution and performance of related services; (e) handling all aspects of order processing, invoicing and collection, inventory and receivables; (f) providing customer support, including handling medical queries, and performing other related functions; and (g) conforming its practices and procedures to Applicable Law relating to the marketing, detailing and promotion of the Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the license granted to Sanofi from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive'. This directly contradicts Section 8.1, which states that Sanofi has the 'sole right and responsibility' for Commercialization of the Products. A non-exclusive license means RevMed could also commercialize, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding commercialization rights.

perturbation.original_text: If the terms of the Research Plan contradict, or create inconsistencies or ambiguities with, the terms of this Agreement, then the terms of this Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.changed_text: If the terms of the Research Plan contradict, or create inconsistencies or ambiguities with, the terms of this Agreement, then the terms of the Research Plan shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes the governing precedence from the original text 'terms of this Agreement shall govern' to 'terms of the Research Plan shall govern'. This now creates an in-text contradiction, potentially leading to disputes over which set of terms should be followed in cases of discrepancies, particularly in areas concerning scope, budgets and responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Committee shall only have the powers expressly assigned to it in this Article II and elsewhere in this Agreement and shall not have the authority to: (a) modify or amend the terms and conditions of this Agreement; (b) waive either Party's compliance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement; or (c) determine any issue in a manner that would conflict with the express terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement precedence. Originally, the agreement terms governed in case of conflict with the research plan. The change prioritizes the Research Plan, directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Committee cannot modify the agreement's terms. By prioritizing the research plan, which the Committee may have influence over, it indirectly allows the Committee to modify the agreement, violating the specified limitations in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: Sanofi will make all payments owed to RevMed within [***] after the date on which Sanofi receives an undisputed invoice for such owed amount, except where a different timeframe is expressly provided in another Section of this Agreement (e.g., for the reimbursement of RevMed R&D Costs pursuant to Sections 4.5 and 5.5; the payment of the buy-in payment pursuant to Section 5.6(b)B; the upfront payment set forth in Section 9.1; the royalties payable pursuant to Section 9.3, the payment of VAT pursuant to Section 9.7(b); and the payment of unpaid or overpaid amounts pursuant to Section 9.9(b)).
perturbation.changed_text: Sanofi will make all payments owed to RevMed within [***] after the date on which Sanofi receives an undisputed invoice for such owed amount, including where a different timeframe is expressly provided in another Section of this Agreement (e.g., for the reimbursement of RevMed R&D Costs pursuant to Sections 4.5 and 5.5; the payment of the buy-in payment pursuant to Section 5.6(b)B; the upfront payment set forth in Section 9.1; the royalties payable pursuant to Section 9.3, the payment of VAT pursuant to Section 9.7(b); and the payment of unpaid or overpaid amounts pursuant to Section 9.9(b)).
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'except where a different timeframe is expressly provided' to 'including where a different timeframe is expressly provided' creates an in-text contradiction within Section 9.5 itself. The original text prioritizes the specific timeframes outlined in other sections (4.5, 5.5, etc.). The revised text implies that the [***] timeframe in 9.5 applies even when other sections specify different timeframes. This perturbation cause conflict with the milestone payments section, where time for milestone payments is not aligned with the [***] in section 9.5. Also created uncertainty around which payment schedule to follow, leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon first achievement of a milestone event described below in this Section 9.2 (a "Milestone Event") by Sanofi or any of its Affiliates or Sublicensees, Sanofi shall notify RevMed of such achievement and RevMed will issue an invoice to Sanofi for the corresponding one- time, non-refundable and non-creditable milestone payment (a "Milestone Payment"). RevMed will also have the right to notify Sanofi in writing if RevMed believes a Milestone Event has been achieved even if Sanofi has not provided such notice to RevMed, and unless Sanofi notifies RevMed within [***] Business Days after receipt of such notice from RevMed that such Milestone Event has not been achieved, RevMed may issue an invoice to Sanofi for the corresponding Milestone Payment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Sanofi will pay to RevMed the following Milestone Payments within [***] after receipt of such invoice therefor as follows:
perturbation.justification: The change from 'except where a different timeframe is expressly provided' to 'including where a different timeframe is expressly provided' in Section 9.5 directly contradicts the timeframe specified for Milestone Payments in Section 9.2. Section 9.2 states that Sanofi will pay Milestone Payments within a specified timeframe after receipt of the invoice. The altered Section 9.5 now implies that the general payment timeframe ([***] after invoice) applies even to Milestone Payments, regardless of what Section 9.2 dictates. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding which payment timeline should be followed, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.
